title: Bloodyad
html:
bloodyAD Collector

bloodyAD supports native BloodHound-compatible data collection without SharpHound or bloodhound-python — useful when those are blocked or unavailable.

Platform: Linux

Quick Start

bloodyAD --host <dc-hostname> -d <domain> -u <username> -p '<password>' get bloodhound

Outputs BloodHound-compatible JSON files in the current directory. Import directly into BloodHound CE via File Ingest.

Authentication Options

# Password
bloodyAD --host <dc-hostname> -d <domain> -u <username> -p '<password>' get bloodhound

# NT hash (pass-the-hash)
bloodyAD --host <dc-hostname> -d <domain> -u <username> -p '<lm-hash>:<nt-hash>' get bloodhound

# Kerberos (ccache)
export KRB5CCNAME=<ccache>
bloodyAD --host <dc-hostname> -d <domain> -k get bloodhound

# LDAPS
bloodyAD --host <dc-hostname> -d <domain> -u <username> -p '<password>' -s get bloodhound

Common Flags

Flag Description
--host DC hostname or IP
-d, --domain Target domain
-u, --username Username
-p, --password Password or LM:NT hash
-k, --kerberos Use Kerberos auth from KRB5CCNAME
-s, --secure Use LDAPS (port 636)
-c, --dc-ip DC IP when DNS is unavailable

Installation

pip install bloodyAD
pipx install bloodyAD
